tool.name: view_image
tool.description: Load an image from the sandbox filesystem and surface it to the agent as a multimodal observation. Use this after a tool writes an image to disk (screenshots saved to a custom path, downloaded evidence, tool-generated diagrams, etc.) when you need to *see* the image rather than describe it via a text tool.
tool.details:
Supported formats: PNG, JPEG, GIF, WebP. The file is base64-encoded and returned on the standard multimodal channel; the executor attaches it to the next agent turn so the vision-capable model can analyze it directly.
Key operational guidance: - Paths resolve inside the sandbox (same filesystem view every other sandbox tool sees). Use absolute paths. - A size cap (default 10 MiB) protects the context window. Oversized images fail with a clear error; run the image through `terminal_execute` + ImageMagick (`convert -resize`) or similar to shrink first. - Prefer this tool over re-rendering HAR / DOM dumps when the finding depends on *visual* state (rendered XSS payload, admin panel, 2FA QR, login captcha, etc.). - Does NOT navigate a browser. Use `browser_goto` + screenshot for live pages; use `view_image` for images already on disk.

parameter.name: path
parameter.description: Absolute path to the image file inside the sandbox (e.g. /workspace/evidence/login-reflected-xss.png)

parameter.name: note
parameter.description: Optional context to attach alongside the image in the observation (e.g. "reflected payload in search field")

parameter.name: max_bytes
parameter.description: Per-call override for the size cap in bytes. Default 10485760 (10 MiB). Values above 20 MiB are clamped.

returns.description: A dict carrying the base64-encoded image on the ``image_base64`` key plus ``mime_type``, ``path``, and ``bytes``. On error the dict contains an ``error`` key and no image payload.

tool.examples:
# Reviewing a screenshot saved to disk by another tool
/workspace/evidence/reflected-xss.png Payload ?q=<svg onload=alert(1)> reflected in the search results
# Looking at a captured login form screenshot
/workspace/evidence/login-form.jpg
